title: Redesign timesheet as calendar UI and fix submission errors for all roles

requirements.summary: Redesign EmployeeTimesheetPage with a modern 7-day calendar grid layout featuring day cards, dark navy/amber theme, and improved visual hierarchy
acceptanceCriteria: The timesheet entry form displays a 7-column calendar grid (Sun–Sat) with one card per day
Each day card contains start time, end time, Building #, and auto-calculated paid hours fields
The layout is visually uncluttered with clear spacing between day cards
The dark navy/amber visual theme is applied consistently throughout the redesigned page
A summary bar below the calendar shows total paid hours for the week and the break deduction note
The Submit Timesheet button is prominently styled and accessible
Previously submitted timesheets are listed below the calendar with status badges
All existing functionality (break deduction, building number, week navigation) is preserved

file_operations.description: Replace the table-based timesheet entry layout with a 7-column calendar grid where each day (Sunday–Saturday) is rendered as an elevated card. Each card should display the day name, numeric date, input fields for start time, end time, Building #, and a read-only display of auto-calculated paid hours (applying the 30-minute break deduction for shifts 8.5+ hours). Apply the dark navy/amber theme with card elevation (bg-white dark:bg-slate-800 shadow-md border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700 rounded-lg p-4), consistent spacing (gap-4 between cards), and clean typography. Add a summary bar below the calendar grid showing the running total paid hours and the break deduction note. Style the Submit Timesheet button prominently (bg-navy-600 hover:bg-navy-700 text-white font-semibold py-3 px-6). Maintain the existing week navigation controls, date selection dropdown, and previously submitted timesheets list with status badges. Ensure all interactive elements (inputs, buttons) remain keyboard-accessible and the layout is responsive.

requirements.summary: Fix employee timesheet submission by auto-deriving employeeId from AuthContext session instead of requiring manual input
acceptanceCriteria: Employees can submit timesheets without any prompt or field for employee ID
The employeeId is read directly from AuthContext session state and passed to the backend
If employeeId is missing from the session, a user-friendly inline warning is shown and submission is blocked
No unhandled promise rejection or console error occurs during timesheet submission
A success toast is shown on successful submission
The submitted timesheet appears in the history list with status 'Pending Manager Review'

file_operations.description: Import useAuth from the AuthContext to access the session state. Before the submit handler runs, check if session.employeeId exists. If it does not exist, display an inline alert (border-l-4 border-amber-500 bg-amber-50 dark:bg-amber-900/20 p-4 text-amber-800 dark:text-amber-200) with the message 'Your account is not linked to an employee profile' and disable the submit button. If employeeId is present, pass it as part of the timesheet payload to the useSubmitTimesheet mutation. Wrap the mutation call in a try/catch and display a success toast ('Timesheet submitted successfully') on success or an error toast with the error message on failure. Ensure the mutation does not require a separate employeeId input field or parameter beyond what is passed from the session.

Update the useSubmitTimesheet mutation's mutationFn to ensure the employeeId is passed as part of the timesheet object payload (not as a separate argument). The mutation should call actor.submitTimesheet(timesheet) where timesheet includes employeeId from the session. Handle the backend response by checking if the result is an #ok or #err variant: if #ok, resolve the promise with the success message; if #err, reject the promise with the error message. Ensure the mutation does not inadvertently require or validate an employeeId field that isn't part of the timesheet object. Add proper type guards to confirm the actor is defined before calling the backend method.

requirements.summary: Fix manager timesheet review submission by ensuring the mutation sends only timesheetId, approved flag, and comment without requiring an employeeId
acceptanceCriteria: Managers can approve or deny submitted timesheets without receiving an 'employee ID' error
The useManagerReviewTimesheet mutation sends timesheet ID, approved flag, and comment only — no extra employee ID field required
The mutation guards against null/undefined actor before calling the backend
Backend #ok/#err variants are correctly mapped to resolved/rejected Promise states
A success toast is shown after manager approves or denies a timesheet
The reviewed timesheet moves from 'Pending Your Review' to 'Reviewed / Processed' immediately after action

file_operations.description: Ensure the manager review dialog and action handlers pass only the timesheetId, approved boolean, and optional comment to the useManagerReviewTimesheet mutation. Do not include or derive an employeeId field in the mutation call. Add a try/catch block around the mutation call and display a success toast ('Timesheet reviewed successfully') on success or an error toast with the backend error message on failure. Ensure the UI immediately updates the timesheet status after a successful review by invalidating the relevant query keys so the timesheet moves from the pending list to the processed list.

Update the useManagerReviewTimesheet mutation's mutationFn to call actor.managerReviewTimesheet(id, approved, comment) where id is the timesheet ID, approved is a boolean, and comment is a string. Add a null/undefined check for the actor before making the call (if (!actor) throw new Error('Actor not available')). Handle the backend response by checking if the result is an #ok or #err variant: if #ok, resolve the promise with the success message; if #err, reject the promise with the error message. Do not pass an employeeId parameter to this backend method. Ensure the mutation invalidates the 'timesheets' query key on success so the UI reflects the updated timesheet status immediately.
